## 🤖 Claude / Anthropic Updates

### Claude 4 Launch (Opus 4 & Sonnet 4)
**Source:** https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-4

Anthropic shipped Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4. Key agent-relevant upgrades:

- **Extended thinking + tool use (beta):** Models can alternate between reasoning and tool calls mid-thought. Enables deeper, more accurate multi-step work.
- **Parallel tool execution:** Both models can now call multiple tools simultaneously (faster research/analysis pipelines).
- **Memory improvements:** When given access to local files, Claude can extract and save key facts to maintain continuity across sessions — exactly how Paw already works with MEMORY.md and daily logs.
- **Sustained multi-hour performance:** Opus 4 can run autonomously for several hours on complex tasks without degradation.
- **SWE-bench:** Sonnet 4 hits 72.7% (state of the art for coding agents).
- Paw is currently running `claude-sonnet-4-6` — already on the Sonnet 4 line.

### New Anthropic Agent API Capabilities
**Source:** https://www.anthropic.com/news/agent-capabilities-api

Four new beta features released alongside Claude 4:

1. **Code execution tool** — Claude can run Python in a sandboxed environment. Relevant for Paw doing FMS financial modeling, data analysis directly.
2. **MCP connector** — Direct connection to any MCP server without custom client code. Could open integrations (Asana, Zapier, etc.) for CEO OS.
3. **Files API** — Upload files once, reference across multiple sessions. Relevant for persistent FMS docs/reports.
4. **1-hour prompt caching** — Reduces cost on long context sessions. Good for heavy briefing sessions with Van.

### Claude Mythos (Project Glasswing)
**Source:** https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing

Anthropic has a new unreleased frontier model called **Mythos** — not publicly available. Deployed only for defensive cybersecurity with 12 partners (AWS, Apple, Google, Microsoft, etc.). Signals where frontier capabilities are heading — sustained complex reasoning well beyond current Sonnet 4.

---

## 🧠 AI Agent Orchestration Trends

### Perplexity Computer — Agent as App Replacement
**Source:** https://www.therundown.ai/p/perplexity-agent-pivot-is-on-the-money

Perplexity's "Computer" agent (launched Feb 2026) has pivoted from search to full personal finance hub — integrated with Plaid (12K+ banks), tax filing, and financial dashboard generation. Their ARR hit $450M, up 50% in one month post-launch.

**Relevance for Paw:** Trend of AI agents becoming the interface layer over top of specialized apps (vs. standalone apps). Paw doing FMS finance via skill is on the right track. The pattern of "agent + data connector + recurring task" is exactly the CEO OS model.

### Notion Agents
**Source:** Rundown guide

Notion now supports custom agents that automate recurring business workflows on schedules (lead triage, accounting, task management). Low-code, schedule-based. Similar to what Paw's cron system already does — but for Notion users.

### Z AI GLM-5.1 (Open Source)
Top open-source coding model (58.4 SWE-Bench Pro) with 8-hour autonomous task capability. Not directly relevant for Paw, but signals open-source catching up fast.

## 💡 Recommended Upgrades for Paw

1. **Tavily top-up (urgent):** Research crons are degraded without it. Single highest-leverage fix this week.
2. **Explore MCP connector:** If OpenClaw gains MCP client support, Paw could connect directly to ClickUp's MCP server — eliminating the custom skill layer.
3. **Consider extended thinking for deep research:** When Anthropic's extended thinking + tool use reaches OpenClaw, enable it for competitor intel and strategic research crons (currently Tavily-dependent).
4. **No major config changes needed this week** — current Paw setup (file-based memory, cron orchestration, Discord delivery) aligns well with where the industry is heading.
